creator: rea@FreeBSD.org
text:
Gustau, good day.

Tue, Feb 14, 2012 at 11:25:12AM +0100, Gustau P?rez wrote:
>    I updated the following ports by removing them and then reinstalling:
> 
>          pkg_info -Ix cups
>          cups-1.5.2          Common UNIX Printing System: Metaport to 
> install complete s
>          cups-base-1.5.2     Common UNIX Printing System: Server
>          cups-client-1.5.2   Common UNIX Printing System: Library cups
>          cups-image-1.5.2    Common UNIX Printing System: Library cupsimage
>          cups-pstoraster-9.0 Postscript interpreter for CUPS printing to 
> non-PS printers
>          gutenprint-cups-5.2.7 GutenPrint Printer Driver
>          ghostscript9-9.05   Ghostscript 9.x PostScript interpreter
> 
>    I had to remove all the .orig files (otherwise it complains about 
> empty patches) in print/cups-pstoraster, with that the files/ directory 
> got empty. After installing pstoraster the print failed with the " 
> /usr/local/libexec/cups/filters/pstoraster failed" message. I noticed 
> that the pstoraster filter was set to 0644, I was not executable. 
> Issuing chmod 0555 to it made it executable.

Had fixed the executable problems and re-rolled the tarball and
the patch: locations are the same, but distinfo was changed to
{{{
SHA256 (cups-pstoraster-9.0.tar.bz2) = 5736cb2427be2b93c1b32adda8cde2b209a347317c5f380e0c1678d59cd2f9cc
SIZE (cups-pstoraster-9.0.tar.bz2) = 5446
}}}

>    I tried with three different printers:
> 
>     - HP 4250 dn
>     - Xerox 6180MFP
>     - Brother HL 2150n (using the HL 1270n cups ppd)
> 
>    After that, printing the sample page with all of these printers 
> works. But if I print any larger job fails with any of those printers. 
> In my error-log I see things like this:
> 
>         W [14/Feb/2012:11:16:24 +0100] [Job 46] Gutenprint detected a 
> bad color depth (1).  Output quality is degraded.  Are you using psnup  
> or non-ADSC PostScript?
> 
>    in my access-log I see:
> 
>        localhost - - [14/Feb/2012:11:15:51 +0100] "POST 
> /printers/Poisson HTTP/1.1" 200 182406 Print-Job successful-ok
> 
>    and in my page_log I see:
> 
>       Poisson gus 46 [14/Feb/2012:11:16:23 +0100] 1 1 - localhost 
> ports/164293: print/cups-pstoraster failed - -
>       Poisson gus 46 [14/Feb/2012:11:16:24 +0100] 2 1 - localhost 
> ports/164293: print/cups-pstoraster failed - -
>       Poisson gus 46 [14/Feb/2012:11:16:24 +0100] 3 1 - localhost 
> ports/164293: print/cups-pstoraster failed - -
[...]

I'll try to understand what's happening.  Could you, please, provide your
CUPS configuration and PPDs you're using along with the port knobs for
the CUPS-related knobs.

Thanks!
-- 
Eygene Ryabinkin                                        ,,,^..^,,,
[ Life's unfair - but root password helps!           | codelabs.ru ]
[ 82FE 06BC D497 C0DE 49EC  4FF0 16AF 9EAE 8152 ECFB | freebsd.org ]
